templates.template: Azure VM Scale Set by HTTP
templates.name: Azure VM Scale Set by HTTP
templates.description:
This template is designed to monitor Microsoft Azure virtual machine scale sets by HTTP.
It works without any external scripts and uses the script item.

Setup:
  1. Create an Azure service principal via the Azure command-line interface (Azure CLI) for your subscription.
    `az ad sp create-for-rbac --name zabbix --role reader --scope /subscriptions/<subscription_id>`
    See https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/cli/azure/create-an-azure-service-principal-azure-cli for more details.
  2. Link the template to a host.
  3. Configure the macros: {$AZURE.APP.ID}, {$AZURE.PASSWORD}, {$AZURE.TENANT.ID}, {$AZURE.SUBSCRIPTION.ID}, and {$AZURE.RESOURCE.ID}.

You can discuss this template or leave feedback on our forum https://www.zabbix.com/forum/zabbix-suggestions-and-feedback/

Generated by official Zabbix template tool "Templator"
templates.readme:
## Overview

This template is designed to monitor Microsoft Azure virtual machine scale sets by HTTP.
It works without any external scripts and uses the script item.

## Setup

1. Create an Azure service principal via the Azure command-line interface (Azure CLI) for your subscription.

      `az ad sp create-for-rbac --name zabbix --role reader --scope /subscriptions/<subscription_id>`

> See [Azure documentation](https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/cli/azure/create-an-azure-service-principal-azure-cli) for more details.

2. Link the template to a host.
3. Configure the host wizard configuration fields: `Azure App ID`, `Azure password`, `Azure tenant ID`, `Azure subscription ID`, and `Azure scale set ID`.

items.name: VM availability metric
items.description: Measure of availability of the virtual machines over time.

items.name: Availability status detailed
items.description: The summary description of availability status.

items.name: Availability state
items.description:
The availability status of the resource.
0 - Available - no events detected that affect the health of the resource.
1 - Degraded  - your resource detected a loss in performance, although it's still available for use.
2 - Unavailable - the service detected an ongoing platform or non-platform event that affects the health of the resource.
3 - Unknown - Resource Health hasn't received information about the resource for more than 10 minutes.

triggers.expression: last(/Azure VM Scale Set by HTTP/azure.scaleset.availability.state)=1
triggers.name: Azure VM Scale: Virtual machine scale set is degraded
triggers.description: The resource is in a degraded state.

triggers.expression: last(/Azure VM Scale Set by HTTP/azure.scaleset.availability.state)=3
triggers.name: Azure VM Scale: Virtual machine scale set is in unknown state

triggers.expression: last(/Azure VM Scale Set by HTTP/azure.scaleset.availability.state)=2
triggers.name: Azure VM Scale: Virtual machine scale set is unavailable

items.name: CPU credits consumed
items.description: Total number of credits consumed by the virtual machine. Only available on B-series burstable VMs.

items.name: CPU credits remaining
items.description: Total number of credits available to burst. Only available on B-series burstable VMs.

items.name: CPU utilization
items.description: The percentage of allocated compute units that are currently in use by the virtual machine(s).

triggers.name: Azure VM Scale: High CPU utilization
triggers.event_name: Azure VM Scale: CPU utilization is high (over {$AZURE.SCALESET.CPU.UTIL.CRIT}% for last 5m)
triggers.description: The CPU utilization is too high. The system might be slow to respond.

items.name: Data disk max burst bandwidth
items.description: Maximum byte-per-second throughput the data disk can achieve with bursting.

items.name: Data disk used burst BPS credits
items.description: Percentage of data disk burst bandwidth credits used so far.

items.name: Data disk bandwidth consumed
items.description: Percentage of data disk bandwidth consumed per minute.

items.name: Data disk target bandwidth
items.description: Baseline byte-per-second throughput the data disk can achieve without bursting.

items.name: Data disk max burst IOPS
items.description: Maximum IOPS the data disk can achieve with bursting.

items.name: Data disk used burst IO credits
items.description: Percentage of data disk burst I/O credits used so far.

items.name: Data disk IOPS consumed
items.description: Percentage of data disk I/Os consumed per minute.

items.name: Data disk target IOPS
items.description: Baseline IOPS the data disk can achieve without bursting.

items.name: Data disk queue depth
items.description: Data disk queue depth (or queue length).

items.name: Data disk read rate
items.description: Bytes/sec read from a single disk during the monitoring period.

items.name: Data disk IOPS read
items.description: Read IOPS from a single disk during the monitoring period.

items.name: Data disk write rate
items.description: Bytes/sec written to a single disk during the monitoring period.

items.name: Data disk IOPS write
items.description: Write IOPS from a single disk during the monitoring period.

items.name: Get errors
items.description: A list of errors from API requests.

triggers.expression: length(last(/Azure VM Scale Set by HTTP/azure.scaleset.data.errors))>0
triggers.name: Azure VM Scale: There are errors in requests to API
triggers.description: Zabbix has received errors in response to API requests.

items.name: Get data
items.description: Gathers data of the virtual machine scale set.

items.name: Disk read
items.description: Bytes read from the disk during the monitoring period.

items.name: Disk IOPS read
items.description: Disk read IOPS.

items.name: Disk write
items.description: Bytes written to the disk during the monitoring period.

items.name: Disk IOPS write
items.description: Write IOPS from a single disk during the monitoring period.

items.name: Inbound flows
items.description: Inbound Flows are the number of current flows in the inbound direction (traffic going into the VMs).

items.name: Inbound flow maximum creation rate
items.description: The maximum creation rate of inbound flows (traffic going into the VM).

items.name: Outbound flows
items.description: Outbound Flows are the number of current flows in the outbound direction (traffic going out of the VMs).

items.name: Outbound flow maximum creation rate
items.description: The maximum creation rate of outbound flows (traffic going out of the VM).

items.name: Network in total
items.description: The number of bytes received on all network interfaces by the virtual machine(s) (incoming traffic).

items.name: Network out total
items.description: The number of bytes out on all network interfaces by the virtual machine(s) (outgoing traffic).

items.name: OS disk used burst BPS credits
items.description: Percentage of OS Disk burst bandwidth credits used so far.

items.name: OS disk bandwidth consumed
items.description: Percentage of operating system disk bandwidth consumed per minute.

items.name: OS disk max burst bandwidth
items.description: Maximum byte-per-second throughput the OS Disk can achieve with bursting.

items.name: OS disk target bandwidth
items.description: Baseline byte-per-second throughput the OS Disk can achieve without bursting.

items.name: OS disk used burst IO credits
items.description: Percentage of OS Disk burst I/O credits used so far.

items.name: OS disk IOPS consumed
items.description: Percentage of operating system disk I/Os consumed per minute.

items.name: OS disk max burst IOPS
items.description: Maximum IOPS the OS Disk can achieve with bursting.

items.name: OS disk target IOPS
items.description: Baseline IOPS the OS disk can achieve without bursting.

items.name: OS disk queue depth
items.description: OS Disk queue depth (or queue length).

items.name: OS disk read rate
items.description: Bytes/sec read from a single disk during the monitoring period - for an OS disk.

items.name: OS disk IOPS read
items.description: Read IOPS from a single disk during the monitoring period - for an OS disk.

items.name: OS disk write rate
items.description: Bytes/sec written to a single disk during the monitoring period - for an OS disk.

items.name: OS disk IOPS write
items.description: Write IOPS from a single disk during the monitoring period - for an OS disk.

items.name: Premium data disk cache read hit in %
items.description: Percentage of premium data disk cache read hit.

items.name: Premium data disk cache read miss in %
items.description: Percentage of premium data disk cache read miss.

items.name: Premium OS disk cache read hit in %
items.description: Percentage of premium OS disk cache read hit.

items.name: Premium OS disk cache read miss in %
items.description: Percentage of premium OS disk cache read miss.

items.name: VM cached bandwidth consumed
items.description: Percentage of cached disk bandwidth consumed by the VM.

items.name: VM cached IOPS consumed
items.description: Percentage of cached disk IOPS consumed by the VM.

items.name: Virtual machine count
items.description: Current amount of virtual machines in the scale set.

triggers.name: Azure VM Scale: High amount of VMs in the scale set
triggers.event_name: Azure VM Scale: Number of VMs is high (over {$AZURE.SCALESET.VM.COUNT.CRIT} for last 5m)
triggers.description: High amount of VMs in the scale set.

items.name: Available memory
items.description: Amount of physical memory, in bytes, immediately available for allocation to a process or for system use in the virtual machine.

items.name: VM uncached bandwidth consumed
items.description: Percentage of uncached disk bandwidth consumed by the VM.

items.name: VM uncached IOPS consumed
items.description: Percentage of uncached disk IOPS consumed by the VM.

macros.description: The App ID of Microsoft Azure.
Sets the HTTP proxy value. If this macro is empty, then no proxy is used.

config.label: HTTP proxy
config.description: HTTP proxy to use for requests.

macros.description: The critical threshold of CPU utilization, expressed in %.

config.label: CPU utilization critical threshold
config.description: Critical threshold of CPU utilization, expressed in %. In the range from 0 to 100 inclusive.

macros.description: The critical amount of virtual machines in the scale set.

config.label: Critical amount of VMs
config.description: Critical amount of virtual machines in the scale set. No less than 0.
